channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Recursive Language Models Meet Uncertainty: The Surprising Effectiveness of Self-Reflective Program Search for Long Context
item.description: arXiv:2603.15653v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-context handling remains a core challenge for language models: even with extended context windows, models often fail to reliably extract, reason over, and use the information across long contexts. Recent works like Recursive Language Models (RLM)...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, RAG, experiment, context, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MedArena: Comparing LLMs for Medicine-in-the-Wild Clinician Preferences
item.description: arXiv:2603.15677v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly central to clinician workflows, spanning clinical decision support, medical education, and patient communication. However, current evaluation methods for medical LLMs rely heavily on static, templated benc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, model, template, GPT, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MiroThinker-1.7 & H1: Towards Heavy-Duty Research Agents via Verification
item.description: arXiv:2603.15726v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present MiroThinker-1.7, a new research agent designed for complex long-horizon reasoning tasks. Building on this foundation, we further introduce MiroThinker-H1, which extends the agent with heavy-duty reasoning capabilities for more reliable mult...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, model, release, context, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Morphemes Without Borders: Evaluating Root-Pattern Morphology in Arabic Tokenizers and LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.15773v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work investigates how effectively large language models (LLMs) and their tokenization schemes represent and generate Arabic root-pattern morphology, probing whether they capture genuine morphological structure or rely on surface memorization. Ara...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, model, product, arxiv, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: COGNAC at SemEval-2026 Task 5: LLM Ensembles for Human-Level Word Sense Plausibility Rating in Challenging Narratives
item.description: arXiv:2603.15897v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We describe our system for SemEval-2026 Task 5, which requires rating the plausibility of given word senses of homonyms in short stories on a 5-point Likert scale. Systems are evaluated by the unweighted average of accuracy (within one standard deviat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, model, RAG, experiment, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BANGLASOCIALBENCH: A Benchmark for Evaluating Sociopragmatic and Cultural Alignment of LLMs in Bangladeshi Social Interaction
item.description: arXiv:2603.15949v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models have demonstrated strong multilingual fluency, yet fluency alone does not guarantee socially appropriate language use. In high-context languages, communicative competence requires sensitivity to social hierarchy, relational roles...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, model, RAG, zero-shot, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: POLAR:A Per-User Association Test in Embedding Space
item.description: arXiv:2603.15950v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most intrinsic association probes operate at the word, sentence, or corpus level, obscuring author-level variation. We present POLAR (Per-user On-axis Lexical Association Re-port), a per-user lexical association test that runs in the embedding space o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, model, embedding, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Family of LLMs Liberated from Static Vocabularies
item.description: arXiv:2603.15953v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tokenization is a central component of natural language processing in current large language models (LLMs), enabling models to convert raw text into processable units. Although learned tokenizers are widely adopted, they exhibit notable limitations, i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, embedding, fine-tuning, release, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Generative AI-assisted Participatory Modeling in Socio-Environmental Planning under Deep Uncertainty
item.description: arXiv:2603.17021v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Socio-environmental planning under deep uncertainty requires researchers to identify and conceptualize problems before exploring policies and deploying plans. In practice and model-based planning approaches, this problem conceptualization process ofte...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, model, template, GPT, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Transformers are Bayesian Networks
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Clued up are LLMs? Evaluating Multi-Step Deductive Reasoning in a Text-Based Game Environment
item.description: arXiv:2603.17169v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deducing whodunit proves challenging for LLM agents. In this paper, we implement a text-based multi-agent version of the classic board game Clue as a rule-based testbed for evaluating multi-step deductive reasoning, with six agents drawn from GPT-4o-m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, GPT, arxiv, LLM, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AI Scientist via Synthetic Task Scaling
item.description: arXiv:2603.17216v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the advent of AI agents, automatic scientific discovery has become a tenable goal. Many recent works scaffold agentic systems that can perform machine learning research, but don't offer a principled way to train such agents -- and current LLMs of...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, model, GPT, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Graph-Native Cognitive Memory for AI Agents: Formal Belief Revision Semantics for Versioned Memory Architectures
item.description: arXiv:2603.17244v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While individual components for AI agent memory exist in prior systems, their architectural synthesis and formal grounding remain underexplored. We present Kumiho, a graph-native cognitive memory architecture grounded in formal belief revision semanti...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, GPT, product, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Contrastive Reasoning Alignment: Reinforcement Learning from Hidden Representations
item.description: arXiv:2603.17305v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose CRAFT, a red-teaming alignment framework that leverages model reasoning capabilities and hidden representations to improve robustness against jailbreak attacks. Unlike prior defenses that operate primarily at the output level, CRAFT aligns ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, model, RAG, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: InfoDensity: Rewarding Information-Dense Traces for Efficient Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.17310v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) with extended reasoning capabilities often generate verbose and redundant reasoning traces, incurring unnecessary computational cost. While existing reinforcement learning approaches address this by optimizing final respon...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, arxiv, framework, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Language to Action in Arabic: Reliable Structured Tool Calling via Data-Centric Fine-Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2603.16901v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Function-calling language models are essential for agentic AI systems that translate natural language into executable structured actions, yet existing models exhibit severe structural instability when applied to Arabic. We present AISA-AR-FunctionCall...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, model, fine-tuning, product, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: What on Earth is AlphaEarth? Hierarchical structure and functional interpretability for global land cover
item.description: arXiv:2603.16911v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Geospatial foundation models generate high-dimensional embeddings that achieve strong predictive performance, yet their internal organization remains obscure, limiting their scientific use. Recent interpretability studies relate Google AlphaEarth Foun...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, embedding, experiment, arxiv, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HoloByte: Continuous Hyperspherical Distillation for Tokenizer-Free Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2603.16917v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sequence modeling universally relies on discrete subword tokenization to circumvent the $\mathcal{O}(N^2)$ computational intractability of native byte-level attention. However, this heuristic quantization imposes artificial morphological boundaries, e...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, embedding, attention, arxiv, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, context window, prompt engineering, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, audio, CoT, framework, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, augmented, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, RAG, framework, LLM, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, framework, LLM, prompt, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Federated Multi Agent Deep Learning and Neural Networks for Advanced Distributed Sensing in Wireless Networks
item.description: arXiv:2603.16881v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent deep learning (MADL), including multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL), distributed/federated training, and graph-structured neural networks, is becoming a unifying framework for decision-making and inference in wireless systems w...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, attention, arxiv, framework, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: CTG-DB: An Ontology-Based Transformation of ClinicalTrials.gov to Enable Cross-Trial Drug Safety Analyses
item.description: arXiv:2603.15936v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: ClinicalTrials .gov (CT .gov) is the largest publicly accessible registry of clinical studies, yet its registry-oriented architecture and heterogeneous adverse event (AE) terminology limit systematic pharmacovigilance (PV) analytics. AEs are typically...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, ICL, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Draft-and-Prune: Improving the Reliability of Auto-formalization for Logical Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.17233v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Auto-formalization (AF) translates natural-language reasoning problems into solver-executable programs, enabling symbolic solvers to perform sound logical deduction. In practice, however, AF pipelines are currently brittle: programs may fail to execut...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, arxiv, framework, reasoning, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Pricing: Balancing Profitability,Stability and Fairness
item.description: arXiv:2603.16888v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Dynamic pricing in competitive retail markets requires strategies that adapt to fluctuating demand and competitor behavior. In this work, we present a systematic empirical evaluation of multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) approaches-specifically...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Formal verification of tree-based machine learning models for lateral spreading
item.description: arXiv:2603.16983v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning models for geotechnical hazard prediction can achieve high accuracy while learning physically inconsistent relationships from sparse or biased training data. Current remedies (post-hoc explainability, such as SHAP and LIME, and traini...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, analysis, paper, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, step-by-step, tool, prompt, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Cascade-Aware Multi-Agent Routing: Spatio-Temporal Sidecars and Geometry-Switching
item.description: arXiv:2603.17112v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A common architectural pattern in advanced AI reasoning systems is the symbolic graph network: specialized agents or modules connected by delegation edges, routing tasks through a dynamic execution graph. Current schedulers optimize load and fitness b...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, reasoning, analysis, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Physics-informed offline reinforcement learning eliminates catastrophic fuel waste in maritime routing
item.description: arXiv:2603.17319v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: International shipping produces approximately 3% of global greenhouse gas emissions, yet voyage routing remains dominated by heuristic methods. We present PIER (Physics-Informed, Energy-efficient, Risk-aware routing), an offline reinforcement learning...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, arxiv, analysis, framework, demonstration | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A foundation model for electrodermal activity data
item.description: arXiv:2603.16878v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foundation models have recently extended beyond natural language and vision to timeseries domains, including physiological signals. However, progress in electrodermal activity (EDA) modeling is hindered by the absence of large-scale, curated, and open...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, model, release, arxiv, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MHPO: Modulated Hazard-aware Policy Optimization for Stable Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.16929v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Regulating the importance ratio is critical for the training stability of Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) based frameworks. However, prevailing ratio control methods, such as hard clipping, suffer from non-differentiable boundaries and vanis...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, framework, reasoning, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Minimum-Action Learning: Energy-Constrained Symbolic Model Selection for Physical Law Identification from Noisy Data
item.description: arXiv:2603.16951v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Identifying physical laws from noisy observational data is a central challenge in scientific machine learning. We present Minimum-Action Learning (MAL), a framework that selects symbolic force laws from a pre-specified basis library by minimizing a Tr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, arxiv, framework, library | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
